_comment: Single provider-routing policy shared by promptcraft, execution-orchestrator, cascade-dispatch, dm-review, assess, and research. Keep task-fit decisions here so provider routing does not drift across plugin docs.

targets.rationale: Execution-provider target: Codex leads at 50 (was 35) since the GPT-5.6 subscription deal (Jul 2026) makes the premium_sub rail flat-rate -- per-token cost is irrelevant there, so it is the cheapest place to spend judgment. Claude drops to 35 (was 50) and still owns ui/integration by chunkKind, where visual and Live Wires judgment is required. OpenRouter holds 15, carrying config, docs, mechanical checks, and large-context synthesis. These three sum to 100 across the chunk-implementation providers. DeepSeek is review-only (mechanical dm-review agents), so it has no execution target here; the post-mortem measures a fourth deepseek providerSplit bucket and compares it against the combined external-provider (openrouter + deepseek) envelope, not against a standalone deepseek target.

chunkKind._comment_kinds: config/docs/mechanical-logic/logic/ui/integration are ROUTING buckets, not manifest kind enum values. The manifest kind enum stays {ui, logic, integration, config}; docs and mechanical-logic are refinements carried by the manifest `executor` field (derived from kind + estimatedComplexity per manifest-schema.md Executor Mapping), not first-class kinds. model-cascade.class_from_kind bridges only the manifest kinds (logic/config/docs/ui/integration); mechanical-logic routes via `executor: openrouter` set at prompt-generation time, so it needs no class_from_kind entry.

config.rationale: Docs, receipts, JSON/YAML/TOML, and pure-prose edits fit cheap large-context text models.

docs.rationale: Documentation generation and cleanup is text-heavy and usually low visual-risk.

mechanical-logic.rationale: Rename follow-through, test tables, seed data, and migration edits are bounded mechanical code changes.

logic.rationale: Complex service and handler logic benefits from Codex agentic coding before falling back to OpenRouter or Claude.

ui.rationale: UI work needs visual judgment, browser verification, and Live Wires design quality review.

integration.rationale: Cross-chunk wiring and route verification need stronger orchestration judgment.

pattern-recognition-specialist.rationale: Pattern checks are mechanical code analysis and do not need Claude unless external providers fail.

code-simplicity-reviewer.rationale: Simplification review is mechanical enough for cheaper review models.

doc-sync-reviewer.rationale: Documentation consistency benefits from large context and cheap prose analysis at any diff size.

test-coverage-reviewer.rationale: Coverage gap detection is read-heavy and works well on large-context models.

openrouter-bulk-analyst.rationale: Bulk/large-context analysis is OpenRouter-primary whenever OPENROUTER_API_KEY is present, not only over a line-count threshold.

security-auditor.rationale: Security remains Claude-native for high-stakes judgment.

architecture-reviewer.rationale: Architecture remains Claude-native for cross-cutting judgment.

assessFanout.rationale: Assessment fan-out is read-heavy summarization; keep final synthesis on Claude.

researchFanout.rationale: Research fan-out is read-heavy summarization; keep final synthesis on Claude.

adversarialReview.rationale: Default adversarial review uses two non-Claude lenses; Claude plan-adversary is an optional third lens or tiebreak.

security._comment: Hard boundary for off-Anthropic delegation (DeepSeek / OpenRouter / GLM). Third-party models are bulk pattern reviewers, NEVER security reviewers. This block overrides chunkKind and agentType routing: any diff matching neverRouteOffAnthropic.pathGlobs, or containing a contentRedaction pattern, is force-routed to Anthropic-side review regardless of what the routing buckets above would select. Consumed by deepseek-agent-runner, deepseek-bulk-analyst, openrouter-bulk-analyst, and the delegate wrapper prompts, which each re-check it as a second layer.

neverRouteOffAnthropic.rationale: Auth, federation trust, secret encryption (secretbox), destructive-confirmation HMAC tokens, email-delivery secrets, deploy secrets, and env files must be reviewed by Anthropic-native seats only. A single matching file in the diff forces the whole chunk Anthropic-side.

refusePatterns: migration or seed files containing credentials
contentRedaction.rationale: The delegate MUST strip or refuse these before sending a diff off-Anthropic. If the diff still contains any of them after stripping, do NOT send it -- return the chunk to Anthropic-side review. This catches secrets that live outside the path globs (e.g. a credential pasted into a migration).

positiveRouting.rationale: These are the lanes DeepSeek / GLM are for. Route them off-Anthropic freely when no security boundary above is tripped.
